title: Capex and Lifecycle Planning
shortSummary: Prioritise building upgrades and plant renewal based on risk, service impact, lifecycle timing, and owner value.
whyItMatters: Lifecycle planning is where day-to-day property knowledge becomes strategic value. Good capex thinking prevents reactive spend, supports better tendering, and helps owners see the trade-offs between delay, risk, cost, and tenant experience. Great property managers can connect a failing asset component to a business case, not just a complaint.
coreConcepts: Risk-based prioritisation instead of first-in, first-out spending
How to build a persuasive capex narrative for owners
The relationship between maintenance history and lifecycle timing
Why capex quality affects leasing, retention, and ESG outcomes
